message.content:
Apply glassmorphism visual changes to these files in C:\Users\User\RickCitedByAstroLab\pages\:
- GenerativeTools.tsx
- Integrations.tsx
- Messages.tsx
- Plan.tsx
- RobotsTxt.tsx
- SchemaOrg.tsx
- SourceOfTruth.tsx
- ActionPlan.tsx
- PublisherRequest.tsx
- Recharge.tsx

CRITICAL RULE: Do NOT modify any functionality, event handlers, state, props, imports, logic, or component structure. ONLY change visual CSS classes/styles.

For EACH file, make these changes:
1. Replace `bg-[#111111]` with `bg-white/[0.04] backdrop-blur-sm` for card/container/input elements
2. Replace `bg-[#1a1d21]` with `bg-white/[0.06] backdrop-blur-xl` for tooltip/overlay elements  
3. Replace `bg-[#13131d]` with `bg-white/[0.04] backdrop-blur-lg` for panel/section backgrounds
4. Replace `bg-[#0f1115]` with `bg-white/[0.03] backdrop-blur-xl` for modal/section backgrounds
5. Do NOT change .ninja-card usages (already handled by CSS globally)
6. Do NOT change any className involving `bg-gradient`, `bg-purple`, `bg-emerald`, `bg-red`, `bg-amber`, `bg-blue`, `bg-green` or other colored backgrounds
7. Do NOT add or remove any elements
8. Keep code blocks/pre dark for readability (`bg-[#0a0a12]`, `bg-[#0a0a0a]` can stay)
9. Do NOT change modal backdrop (`bg-black/80`, `bg-black/60`)

Be conservative - if uncertain about a change, skip it.

content.text: I'll start by reading all 10 files to understand their current content before making changes.

content.text: All target background classes have been successfully replaced across all 10 files. Now let me also check for `border-[#111111]` instances which were in Messages.tsx (used on online indicator borders). These are border colors, not backgrounds, so per the rules I should skip them. Let me verify they are just border colors.

content.content:

397:              <span className="absolute -bottom-0.5 -right-0.5 w-3 h-3 bg-green-500 rounded-full border-2 border-[#111111]" />

content.text:
These are border colors on the online status indicator dots (green dots with a border to create a "cutout" effect against the background). Since the instructions specify only replacing `bg-[#111111]` (background classes) and these are `border-[#111111]`, they fall outside the scope of the requested changes. The instruction says "Replace `bg-[#111111]`" specifically. I'll skip these as they are not background classes.

Let me also quickly verify that no `bg-[#000000]` instances need handling. The instructions mention keeping code blocks/pre dark (`bg-[#0a0a12]`, `bg-[#0a0a0a]` can stay), and `bg-[#000000]` appears to serve a similar function in code/preview areas and dark table headers. The rules don't list `bg-[#000000]` as a target for replacement, so those are safe to leave.
